senses.gloss: to take (into account), to take (into consideration), to reckon with
to assume, to think, to suppose, to believe

examples.text: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّٰهَ غَافِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ الظَّالِمُونَ
examples.english: Do not think that God is unaware of what the wrongdoers do.

senses.gloss: to regard, to consider, to deem

examples.english: I've only read three books (and that's all).

examples.text: الغالبية العظمى من المسلمين لا يؤمنون فحسب بأن الله رحيم؛ بل يؤمنون بأن الله يدعو البشر إلى الرحمة أيضًا.
examples.english: The vast majority of Muslims not only believe that God is merciful; they believe that God calls humans to be merciful as well.

etymology.text:
Cognate with Hebrew חָשַׁב (khasháv), Classical Syriac ܚܫܒ, and Egyptian ḥsb.

Adverbial accusative of حَسَب (ḥasab).
